question: Looking at the US unemployment rate map, which statement below is the most accurate?

options.A: The unemployment rate differences are impossible to determine from this visualization.
options.B: All states shown have almost identical unemployment rate levels.
options.C: The unemployment rate gap between Delaware and Iowa might exceed that between Michigan and Maine.
options.D: Delaware and Iowa have similar unemployment rates.

explanation: Delaware (9.6%) and Iowa (15.6%) have a 6.0% unemployment rate difference, while Michigan (2.9%) and Maine (3.5%) have only a 0.6% difference. However, the categorical color encoding groups Delaware and Iowa into the same color bin (9%+ range), making their large difference appear similar, while Michigan and Maine appear more different due to different color bins.
